data.title: In 2025, NFT marketplaces like OpenSea and Magic Eden adapted to survive, evolving as the NFT hype faded. Learn more.
data.description: OpenSea and Magic Eden have experienced significant growth over the past year, even as the NFT frenzy has faded into the background. OpenSea, a popular NFT marketplace, saw a 200% increase in daily active users, reaching over 1.5 million. In comparison, Magic Eden, another NFT platform, reported a 150% surge in transactions, totaling more than $500 million in sales. Despite the waning interest in NFTs, both platforms have managed to thrive and adapt to the evolving market dynamics.

data.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum, XRP, Dogecoin prices steady as Bitcoin hits $87,000. 93,477 traders liquidated in 24 hrs for $244.46M. Spot Bitcoin ETFs saw $175.3M outflows.
data.description: Heading into the final weekend of 2025, Bitcoin remained mostly level around $87,000 as investors exercised caution due to low trading volume and selling driven by taxes. The latest cryptocurrency prices were Bitcoin at $87,351.16, Ethereum at $2,923.07, Solana at $122.01, XRP at $1.84, Dogecoin at $0.1221, and Shiba Inu at $0.057119. Recent data from Coinglass revealed that in the past 24 hours, 93,477 traders were liquidated, resulting in losses totaling $244.46 million. Additionally, SoSoValue data indicated net outflows of $175.3 million from spot Bitcoin ETFs on Wednesday.

data.title: Azazie to host one-day bridal pop-up in San Diego on Dec. 27, 2025, dresses up to 85% off.
data.description: On December 27, 2025, Azazie will be hosting a pop-up shopping event in San Diego, bringing their sought-after shopping experience to brides-to-be and bridal parties. Attendees will have the opportunity to explore and try on wedding, bridesmaid, and formal dresses in person. This exclusive event will cater to individuals looking for the perfect dress for their special occasion.

data.title: Telcoin launches eUSD stablecoin after approval; minted $10 million, aimed at retail markets on Ethereum and Polygon.
data.description: Telcoin Digital Asset Bank recently received approval from the Nebraska Department of Banking and Finance to begin its blockchain banking operations. As a result, Telcoin has launched its eUSD stablecoin on both the Ethereum and Polygon blockchains. The minting of $10 million worth of eUSD marks a significant milestone for Telcoin, bringing its flagship Digital Cash stablecoin closer to retail markets. The company is excited to kick off this initiative and is moving forward with its plans to expand further.
